<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

A naturalist peasant tragedy set in a rural Russian village. A weak-willed farmhand named Nikita falls under the influence of his manipulative mother and is drawn step by step into adultery, poisoning, and infanticide. The play traces a moral descent with the relentless logic of classical tragedy transposed into a peasant setting. Banned from the Imperial stage before its Russian premiere, it was first performed in Paris in 1888. Tolstoy called it a depiction of how one sin leads inevitably to the next.
